summary: Top-level /templates/ directory is a stale, unused duplicate of agentteams/templates/ (only 9 constitutional rules, Workflows 1-8) with zero readers anywhere in the pipeline; risks a contributor editing the wrong copy with no effect.
proposed_touch_points: /templates/ (delete or clearly mark legacy); consider a CI check asserting only agentteams/templates/ is read by the pipeline

summary: 3 of 4 universal *.csv.template static files (adjacent-repos-changelog, adjacent-repos-coordination-log, security-decisions.log) have zero readers anywhere in agentteams/**/*.py; the real mechanism is liaison_logs.py's Python constants, creating a duplicated-truth risk if the two ever diverge.

agentteams --dry-run --json does not emit a single parseable JSON document on stdout as --help implies: it prints ~9 lines of human-readable progress text to stdout FIRST, then the JSON payload after — the combined stream is not valid JSON even though the JSON object itself is well-formed once isolated.

A successful --update --merge run's console summary ('No structural or content changes detected...Written: 0 file(s)') can be misleading: confirmed real template-driven content changes were written to disk despite this message, and the printed file count did not match the dry-run's own plan count for the same inputs.

examples/*/expected/ golden snapshots silently drift from the template library over time with no CI check or regeneration reminder; --update --merge alone cannot restore fresh-render parity once pre-existing shrink-guard-suppressed fences have diverged, only --overwrite can, which is security-gated.
proposed_touch_points: tests/test_integration.py::test_snapshot_comparison; a periodic/CI snapshot-freshness check; docs_src/contributing.md regeneration recipe

summary: Two distinct security gates (destructive-action overwrite gate and security-intelligence-freshness gate) both print near-identical 'Security gate blocked ...:' messages, making it easy for an operator to misdiagnose which gate fired and what actually unblocks it.

agentteams --update --merge never re-plans the instructions file (copilot-instructions.md / CLAUDE.md-equivalent) for an existing team, regardless of framework -- only fresh generation includes it in the effective output plan. Confirmed two ways: (1) .github/copilot-instructions.md's mtime was untouched by today's --self --update --merge run despite unrelated fenced content elsewhere changing; (2) a --dry-run --json plan for --framework claude --output .claude/agents --update --merge listed 46 entries and none was '../CLAUDE.md', even though the original from-scratch build-log.json's output_files_map explicitly includes it. Any project relying on --update --merge to keep its instructions file current (not just its per-agent files) will silently drift -- exactly the kind of staleness this session had to hand-fix in both .github/agents/orchestrator.agent.md's and .claude/CLAUDE.md's Constitutional Rules sections.
proposed_touch_points: agentteams/cli/generate.py or agentteams/render.py (update-mode file planning); output_plan.py

summary: references/bridges/<pair>/bridge-manifest.json embeds an absolute source_dir path (confirmed pre-existing via git history in a real consumer repo, predating this session), the same class of finding already fixed twice this session (cli/artifacts.py delivery-receipt; bridge_subagents.py/_goose.py stub bodies). Not fixed here -- flagging as a 4th, not-yet-investigated location for a future pass over agentteams/bridge.py's manifest/report generation to check for the same pattern.
